summary: In her acceptance comments for the 2020 Breakthrough Prize, Virginia M.-Y. Lee reflects on her unexpected career path from studying music to neuroscience. She highlights the importance of her foundational training in biochemistry and credits her mentors, collaborators, and trainees for their roles in her discoveries regarding neurodegenerative diseases.

signature_quotes.text: Growing up in Hong Kong in a very traditional Chinese family, I never thought of becoming a scientist let alone receiving such a distinguished honor.
signature_quotes.context: Reflecting on her early life and unexpected career path.

signature_quotes.text: I left music for science and ended up completing my PhD thesis research... where I received outstanding training in the biochemistry of protein extractions and protein purifications. This skill set turned out to be extremely useful in my efforts to identify some of the proteins involved in Alzheimer’s, Parkinson’s, frontotemporal dementia and ALS, in my independent career.
signature_quotes.context: On the long-term value of her foundational biochemical training.
